name: Black Bean Stuffed Peppers
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup frozen Florida orange juice concentrate, thawed
15 ounces black beans, rinsed and drained
11 ounces whole kernel corn with black beans
3/4 cup shredded reduced-fat Monterey Jack cheese
recipeInstructions:
1. Fill a Dutch oven about half full of water; bring to boiling.
2. Halve peppers lengthwise, removing seeds and membranes. Add peppers to boiling water-and return to boiling; reduce heat. Cover and simmer for 4 to 5 minutes or until peppers are just tender. Drain peppers.
3. Meanwhile, in a medium saucepan combine broth, thawed concentrate, onion, uncooked rice, and chili powder. Bring to boiling; reduce heat. Cover and simmer about 20 minutes or until rice is tender. Stir in beans and corn.
4. Stir in most of the cheese.
5. Fill peppers with rice mixture. Place in a 3-quart rectangular baking dish.
6. Bake, covered, in a 400°F oven for 25 minutes or until heated through. Sprinkle with remaining cheese. If desired, garnish with orange wedges.
7. Makes 4 servings (2 pepper-halves).
